text: Shree Saibaba Sansthan Trust, Shirdi, is the Governing and
Administrative body of the Shree Saibaba's Samadhi Temple and all others temples on
this premise, and devoted towards teachings of Saibaba “Sabka Malik Ek” and providing
amenities to Sai devotees who are visiting Shirdi and Sai Samadhi Temple.
Shree Saibaba Sansthan Trust, Shirdi, is the authorized body to control and manage the
day-to-day activities at the Shree Saibaba Samadhi Temple. It also provides various
facilities like Accommodation, Meals (Free), refreshments, and lot more. The Sansthan
Trust also runs School and Colleges (Junior and Senior), English Medium School from
class Jr KG to Std X, Industrial Training Institute (ITI), Drinking Water Supply, Hospitals
(Shree Saibaba Superspecility Hospital and Shree Sainath Hospital on Charity basis).
Shirdi, a small rural town in the Rahata Taluka of Ahilyanagar district in the Maharashtra
state of India. Shirdi is a secular place where all religions are treated as one & a belief in
the power of Faith & Patience is foremost. A place where all heads bow down in prayer,
where faith prevails, where hopes are built, where patience pays, and where infinite joy
and everlasting contentment abound. Such is the glory of the place which belongs to the
Holy Saint, a true repository of wisdom, who pleased all with pious equality and gifted
mankind ornaments of humanity and peace by saying "
footprints of Saibaba have made this town a holy place. Millions of devotees
continuously flock here from all over India and abroad.
Shirdi is where the holy soul of
” rests today. Shree Saibaba appeared at Shirdi in his
human incarnation. For 60 years Baba served mankind and preached his precious
teachings to the world from here, and then went for contemplation (Samadhi) in Shirdi
itself. The footprints and the deeds of Saibaba have turned this small town into a unique
holy place for people of all castes, creeds and religions. Saibaba delivered the universal
slogan of "Shraddha - Saburi", i.e. Faith and Patience for everyone, from here itself. The
place of his 'Samadhi' has become a center-point for innumerable devotees, infusing
them with the joy of life. While visiting Shirdi, one experiences complete peace of mind,
a strong self-confidence, and a great sense of purpose. During his contemplation
(Samadhi) services, Shree Saibaba had consoled his devotees with these words - "After I
take Samadhi, my bones will speak from the grave, and people will throng here." His
message is being experienced till today. Shirdi is a convenient place to visit throughout
the year, during all seasons. Baba's Shirdi is one place where even today all his devotees
come with empty hands, but filled with Hope, and go back "Blessed with Contentment
with an Everlasting Smile on their faces".
The Shree Saibaba Sansthan Trust in Shirdi is currently overseen by an Ad-Hoc
Committee appointed by the Honourable Bombay High Court, Sambhajinagar Bench.
This Ad-Hoc Committee is responsible for the management and administration of the
Trust, ensuring its operations align with legal and ethical standards. The committee's
formation underscores the importance of judicial oversight in the governance of
The committee comprises the following members:
Shree Saibaba Sansthan Trust, Shirdi, PO: Shirdi Tal. Rahata Dist.
Shree Saibaba Sansthan Trust, Shirdi is situated on the Ahilyanagar-Manmad highway.

Places to See in and around Shirdi:
Here are the one-line briefs about the places in Shirdi:
- in Shirdi is where Sai Baba was first seen under a neem tree, symbolizing the
". It is a sacred spot with a temple, a Shiva Linga, and the tree, where
devotees pray for blessings and good health.
- in Shirdi is the sacred mosque where Sai Baba lived for over 60 years,
guiding and blessing his devotees. It is considered a symbol of unity, housing his sacred
fire (Dhuni) and other relics of his life.
- in Shirdi is a sacred place where Sai Baba used to spend alternate nights during
his later years. It is now a temple and holds great significance for devotees, symbolizing
- in Shirdi is a peaceful garden where Sai Baba used to meditate and water
plants. It houses sacred spots like the Nanda Deep (a continuously burning lamp) and is
a place of devotion and serenity for devotees.
- in Shirdi is a temple dedicated to Lord Hanuman, where Sai
Baba often visited. Devotees come here to pray for strength, courage, and blessings.
in Shirdi that are dedicated to lord Ganesh, Shani (i.e. Saturn) and

Dwarakamai and said to have received mukti from Baba, is a few feet from the
- in Shirdi is a prominent temple dedicated to Goddess Mahalaxmi,
the deity of wealth and prosperity. Devotees visit the temple to seek blessings for
in Shirdi is dedicated to Lord Narasimha, an incarnation of Lord
Vishnu. It is a revered site where devotees pray for protection, strength, and the
removal of obstacles in their lives.
in Shirdi is dedicated to Lord Parshvanatha, the 23rd Tirthankara of
Jainism. It is an important pilgrimage site for Jain devotees, known for its peaceful
in Shirdi is a sacred eternal lamp kept burning near the Gurusthan,

Here are the one-line briefs about the places around Shirdi:
- Shani Shingnapur is a famous temple village in Maharashtra, known
for its dedication to Lord Shani, the deity of justice. The unique aspect of the temple is
that it has no doors, as it is believed that Lord Shani himself protects the village from
- Muktidham in Nashik is a spiritual temple complex dedicated to Lord
Krishna. It is renowned for its replicas of 12 Jyotirlingas and other important Hindu
Shreenes, offering devotees a chance to experience the essence of various sacred sites
- Kalaram Temple in Nashik is an ancient and revered Hindu temple
dedicated to Lord Rama. It is known for its historical significance and houses a black
stone idol of Lord Rama, attracting devotees who come to seek blessings and peace.
- Pandavleni Caves in Nashik are ancient Buddhist caves, carved into
hills that were used for meditation and prayer. They are famous for their detailed
carvings and inscriptions from the 3rd century BCE.
- Ramkund in Nashik is a sacred bathing ghat located on the banks of the
Godavari River. It is believed to be the place where Lord Rama took a bath during his
exile, making it an important pilgrimage spot for devotees.
- Ellora Caves, located near Sambhajinagar, Maharashtra, are a complex of
34 rock-cut caves known for their impressive architecture and intricate sculptures.
These caves include Buddhist, Hindu, and Jain temples, dating back to between the 5th
and 10th centuries, highlighting ancient religious and cultural heritage.
- Daulatabad, near Sambhajinagar, is a historic fort known for its strategic
location and impressive architecture. It was once the capital of the Delhi Sultanate and
is famous for its complex defences, including a hidden entrance and a deep moat.
- the Coin Museum in Nashik is dedicated to the history and evolution of
coins in India. It highlights a vast collection of ancient and medieval coins, helping
visitors understand the cultural and economic significance of coinage throughout
Trimbakeshwar, located near Nashik, is a famous Hindu temple
dedicated to Lord Shiva. It is one of the twelve Jyotirlingas and is known for its spiritual
significance and unique architectural style, attracting numerous devotees every year.
- Alandi, located near Pune, is a revered pilgrimage town known for the Samadhi
of Saint Dnyaneshwar. It is an important site for devotees of the Varkari sect, who visit
to pay respects and seek blessings from the saint, who contributed to the spiritual and
- Nevasa, located in the Ahilyanagar district of Maharashtra, is a historical town
known for its ancient temples and cultural significance. It is especially famous for the
Khandoba Temple and its association with Maratha history and local legends.
- Bhimashankar, located in the Western Ghats near Pune, is an important
Hindu pilgrimage site known for the Bhimashankar Temple dedicated to Lord Shiva. It is
one of the twelve Jyotirlingas and is renowned for its serene environment, surrounded
- Shegaon, located in Maharashtra, is a small town known for the Samadhi of
Saint Gajanan Maharaj, a revered spiritual leader. The Gajanan Maharaj Temple attracts
thousands of devotees who seek blessings for peace, prosperity, and spiritual growth.

Sansthan accepts donation from devotees in various modes like Cash, Cheque, Demand
Draft, Money Order, Debit/Credit Card, Foreign Currency and In-kind. Devotees can get
the donation receipt, Udi Prasad and special privileges by offering donations at Sansthan
Donation Counters in the Temple premises, Information Centers at 4 different cities, by
post, Online portal & Android Mobile App and direct Bank to Bank transfers. Also,
Devotees can donate anonymously through Donation Boxes (Hundi) in the Sansthan
Here are the various modes of Donation available:
Around 15 computerized Donation counters are available in Temple premises and 4
Donation counters (Information Centers) are available at Mumbai, Chennai, Hyderabad
and Bangalore, for detail address of this office visit Information Centers.
Donation counters accept Cash, Cheque, Demand Draft, Debit/Credit Card, Foreign
The Donation receipts for Cash and kind donations are given immediately and for other
kind of donations, the donation receipts are sent by post within 15 days.
A devotee can donate only Rs-1, 99,999/- (Rs. One Lakh ninety-nine thousands Nine
hundred ninety-nine) in Cash in single day.
Section 80G of Income Tax act will not be applicable for Cash donation more than
Devotee donating in foreign Currency have to submit self-attested Xerox copy of
Cheque, Money Order or DD should be drawn in the favour of Shree Saibaba Sansthan
Trust, Shirdi and should be sent to the following address:
The donation receipt, along with holy Udi-Prasad, will be sent to the devotee's address

Donation in between 10000 to 24999 will have the privileges of either one free VIP Aarti
or one free VIP Darshan for a maximum of 5 devotees, along with 5 Udi packets and 1
Donation in between 25000 to 50000 will have the privileges of either one free VIP Aarti
and one free VIP Darshan or Two free VIP Aarti’s or Two free VIP Darshan’s for a
maximum of 5 devotees, along with 5 Udi packets, 2 Laddu’s, 1 3D pocket photo, 1
Donation in between 50001 to 99999 will have the privileges of either Two free VVIP
Aarti’s or Two free VVIP Darshan’s for a maximum of 5 devotees, along with 5 Udi
packets and 2 Laddu’s, 1 3D pocket photo, 1 Shree Satcharitra Book.
Donation in between 100000 to 999999 will have the special privileges. For VVIP
Darshan, the donor gets 2 free passes in the first year. After that, 1 pass is added every
year, and this continues until the number of years reaches the donation amount divided
by 1 lakh (for example: if the donation is ₹3,00,000, then 3 years). The same rule applies
for VVIP Aarti—2 free passes in the first year, and then 1 extra pass each year up to the
number of years equal to donation ÷ 1 lakh. For VIP Darshan, the donor will get 1 free
pass every year for a lifetime. For VIP Aarti, the passes depend on the donation amount
(example: ₹1 lakh = 1 pass, ₹3 lakhs = 3 passes) and these passes can be used for a
lifetime. For Pooja, the donor gets 1 free lifetime pass, which can be used anytime.
Along with 5 Udi packets and 3 Laddu’s, 1 3D pocket photo, 1 Shree Satcharitra Book, 1
3D Desk note holder, 1 Sanman Shawl, and 1 Abhishek / Satyanarayana Coupon.
Donation in between 10 Lakhs to 50 Lakhs will have the following privileges
Darshan, the donor receives 2 passes in the first year, and from the following year
onward, 1 extra pass is added every year for a lifetime. For VVIP Aarti, the number of
year passes is calculated by dividing the donation amount by ₹10 lakhs and then
multiplying the result by 2 (for example, a ₹20
÷ 10 = 2, and 2 × 2 = 4). From the next year onward, 1 pass will be added every year for a
lifetime. For Pooja, the donor gets 1 lifetime pass that can be used anytime. For Cloth
Offering, the donor receives 1 pass, and its validity in years depends on the donation
amount—specifically, the number of valid years equals the multiple of every ₹10 lakhs
donated (for example, ₹30 lakhs = 3 years of validity). Along with 5 Udi packets, 5
Laddu’s, 1 3D pocket photo, 1 Shree Satcharitra Book, 1 3D Desk note holder, 1 Sai Baba
Statue, 1 Sanman Shawl, Shree Sai Baba weared cloth, and 1 Abhishek / Satyanarayana
Donation which is more than 50 Lakhs will have the following privileges For VVIP
Darshan, the donor receives 2 passes in the first year, and from the next year onward, 3
passes will be added every year for a lifetime. For VVIP Aarti, the donor gets 3 passes in
the first year, and then 3 additional passes are added every year for a lifetime. For
Pooja, the donor receives 1 pass, which can be used at any time throughout their
lifetime. For Cloth Offering, the donor gets 1 pass, and its validity in years depends on
the donation amount—the number of valid years is equal to the multiple of every ₹10
lakhs donated. Along with 5 Udi packets, 5 Laddu’s, 1 3D pocket photo, 1 Shree
Satcharitra Book, 1 3D Desk note holder, 1 Sai Baba Statue, 1 Sanman Shawl, Shree Sai
Baba weared cloth, and 1 Abhishek / Satyanarayana Coupon.

Here are the various types of Pooja available:
Shree Sai Satyavrata Puja According to Chapter 13 of Shree Sai Satcharita, a devotee
named Bhimaji Patil from Narayangaon was suffering from a chronic illness. Despite
undergoing various medical treatments and religious rituals, he did not find relief from
his ailment. Eventually, he sought refuge in Shree Saibaba based on the advice of
Nanasaheb Chandorkar. With the blessings and grace of Saibaba, his illness was cured.
Bhimaji Patil was a devotee of Lord Narayana (Lord Vishnu), and after experiencing the
healing powers of Saibaba, he realized that Baba is the incarnation of the Supreme God,
Shree Narayana. This led to the initiation of a new tradition of worship called "Shree Sai
Satyavrata Puja." As a result, the worship of Saibaba's idol is now performed during the
Satyanarayana Puja to fulfil the desires of Sai devotees. In the concluding chapters of
the Satyanarayana Puja, Bhimaji Patil provides a brief description of his experience.

Darshan at the Sai Baba Temple in Shirdi is a deeply spiritual experience, with devotees
seeking blessings at the Samadhi Mandir, where Sai Baba’s idol resides. The temple's
serene atmosphere is enhanced by devotional chants and organized darshan queues.
Free Darshan at Shirdi allows devotees to visit and seek blessings of Shree Sai Baba
without any charges. The queue system is well organized, and facilities such as drinking
You can also opt for special darshan passes or services if needed for a quicker Darshan

audio-visual media, calendars, and guides for devotees. These publications are available
at the temple, online, and authorized centers to deepen spiritual connections.

The KIOSK machines installed by Sansthan are available at the Shree Sai Baba Temple in Shirdi is
a self-service facility designed to simplify the process of obtaining VIP Darshan Passes and VIP
meal tickets for devotees. These machines provide a user-friendly, queue-free experience,
allowing visitors to generate their passes independently without having to stand in manual
The primary objective of the kiosk machines is to make the VIP Darshan pass and VIP meal
ticket booking faster, more convenient, and accessible to all devotees visiting the temple. With
tens of thousands of people visiting Shirdi daily, these kiosks help reduce congestion and
Devotees start by entering their basic details on the kiosk screen. The interface is simple
and intuitive, ensuring that even first-time users can handle the process easily.
Once the details are submitted, the kiosk generates a UPI QR code for payment.
UPI QR is the only accepted payment method at these kiosks.
Debit cards, credit cards, and cash payments are not supported.
After successful UPI payment, the kiosk automatically prints a VIP Darshan pass / VIP
meal ticket receipt, which the devotee can use at the temple’s designated entry gate.
To accommodate the large number of devotees, the Sansthan has made kiosk machines
are installed here, serving as the primary spot for quick

Can I Book Room for Single Person?
No, Room booking can be allowed to book for Minimum of 2 persons & Maximum of 5
Can I make Online Donation in Foreign Currency?
No, Online Donation Accepts only Indian Rupees.
What is the meaning of colours given to the dates in the calendar?
Dates having green colour are available for booking. Dates with grey colour means
quota is not released for booking or the dates have been already passed. Dates with red
colour means quota is full for those dates.
How early and how late can I book a room online before my arrival date in Shirdi?
You can book a room up to 60 days in advance, including for the current day, subject to
After booking the room, can I change Check-in and Check-Out dates?
Once a room is booked, you cannot change the check-in/check-out date. If you want to
postpone your check in time, you can call helpdesk and request for postponement of
check in time. However, check in time cannot be extended beyond check out time.
How many open transactions are allowed for online room booking?
In one login account, only 1 open transaction is allowed. Only after you have availed the
existing booking, you may proceed for another booking.
Can I change my accommodation room type after I have booked the room?
No, you cannot change the accommodation details after the booking has been made. In
case you wish to do so, you can cancel the existing booking and proceed with a new
booking. Appropriate cancellation charges will apply as per the Terms & Conditions.
While booking accommodation, I am getting the error message "No Search results
If an error message "No Search Results found" appears while booking the
accommodation rooms, it means that no rooms are available for the search criteria
How check out date is calculated?
Your reservation date is 21st May 2024, with check-in time slot selected as 08:00 AM-
11:59 AM & number of days as 1/2 day(s). Then the check-out date & time will be
24hrs/48hrs from check in date & time slot. For 1 day check-out date & time is 22nd
May 2024 & 8:00 AM. For 2 days check-out date and time is 23rd May 2024 & 8:00 AM.
How can I extend my stay after check out time?
You have to report to the local counter in Shirdi. Depending on availability of rooms, you
will be granted room extension after payment of room extension charges.
Can I book a room on behalf of other persons?
You cannot book a room on behalf of any other person. Room booking cannot be
transferred to other person. The person on whose name room is booked should be
present while availing the accommodation in Shirdi with her/his photo id and
Can I change the date or time slot when I booked my Darshan / Aarti?
No. You cannot change Darshan/Aarti date and time slot.
How many open transactions are allowed for online booking of Darshan facility?
In one login account, only 1 open transaction is allowed. Only after you have availed the
existing booking, you may proceed for another booking.
Can I book a Darshan facility on behalf of other person?
Yes. You can book a Darshan or Aarti facility on behalf of any other person. However,
the person on whose name Darshan facility is booked should be present while availing
the pass in Shirdi with her/his photo Id and acknowledgement.
How early and how late can I book Darshan and Aarti online before my arrival date in
You can book Aarti maximum 30 days in advance and minimum 2 days in advance
before your arrival in Shirdi. In addition, you can book Darshan from t +2 hours until 30
days in advance based on the availability, where t is the current hour.
Can I donate for multiple funds in one transaction?
Yes. You can donate for multiple funds in one transaction.
Will I get receipt for donation?
Yes. You will get receipt within 60 days on your registered address with udi-prasad.
